senses.definition: plural of pyrolysate, a chemical substance produced by pyrolysis (the breakdown of materials by heat)
examples: The reactor yielded several pyrolysates as the plastic decomposed.
Researchers identified compounds among the pyrolysates formed during pyrolysis.

senses.definition: the process of pyrolysis; the decomposition of substances by heat to form simpler compounds
examples: The material was treated by pyrolyse to study its breakdown products.
Pyrolyse occurred at high temperatures in the absence of oxygen.

senses.definition: to cause or undergo pyrolysis; to thermally decompose a substance
examples: The sample was pyrolyse to analyze its components.
Materials pyrolyse under high heat in inert conditions.

senses.definition: past tense of pyrolyze; having undergone pyrolysis
examples: The polymer was pyrolysed to study its decomposition products.
Materials pyrolysed at high temperatures show different char structures.

senses.definition: a device or person that performs pyrolysis or facilitates thermal decomposition
examples: The lab used a pyrolyser to break down samples.
An automated pyrolyser heated the material to initiate pyrolysis.

senses.definition: plural of pyrolyser; devices or agents that perform pyrolysis
examples: Different pyrolysers were tested for efficiency.
The collection of pyrolysers enabled parallel experiments.

senses.definition: plural of pyrolysis; processes of thermally decomposing substances
examples: Various organic pyrolyses occur under different heat conditions.
The yields depend on the details of the pyrolyses performed.

senses.definition: present participle of pyrolyze; undergoing pyrolysis or causing thermal decomposition
examples: The sample is pyrolysing at 600°C.
They are pyrolysing several polymers to compare product distributions.

senses.definition: chemical decomposition of a substance by heat
examples: Pyrolysis breaks down plastics into simpler chemicals.
The byproducts come from the pyrolysis of organic matter.

senses.definition: relating to pyrolysis or produced by pyrolysis
examples: Pyrolytic carbon is formed during high-temperature processing.
The pyrolytic products were analyzed for contaminants.

senses.definition: in a manner related to pyrolysis; by means of pyrolysis
examples: The compound was heated pyrolytically to study its breakdown products.
Pyrolytically treated samples showed different mineral content.

senses.definition: capable of undergoing pyrolysis
examples: Many polymers are pyrolyzable at sufficiently high temperatures.
Not all materials are pyrolyzable under the same conditions.

senses.definition: a substance produced by pyrolysis; a pyrolysis product
examples: The pyrolyzate mixture was analyzed to identify components.
New pyrolyzates were detected during the process.

senses.definition: plural of pyrolyzate; substances produced by pyrolysis
examples: Researchers collected several pyrolyzates from the reactor.
Different pyrolyzates indicate different decomposition pathways.

senses.definition: to heat a substance strongly so it decomposes
examples: We will pyrolyze the sample to study its components.
The material is heated to pyrolyze under inert gas.

senses.definition: past tense of pyrolyze; has been heated to decompose
examples: The sample was pyrolyzed to reveal its composition.
Pyrolyzed products were analyzed further.

senses.definition: a device or person that performs pyrolysis
examples: The pyrolyzer heated the sample to initiate decomposition.
An automated pyrolyzer speeds up analysis.

senses.definition: plural of pyrolyzer; devices or people that perform pyrolysis
examples: Several pyrolyzers were tested in parallel.
The lab added more pyrolyzers to increase throughput.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of pyrolyze; decomposes by heat
examples: The sample pyrolyzes under the applied heat.
These materials pyrolyzes into simpler compounds.

senses.definition: present participle of pyrolyze; undergoing pyrolysis
examples: The mixture is pyrolyzing at high temperature.
They are pyrolyzing various polymers to compare products.

senses.definition: a war or fighting involving fire; a fighting style emphasizing fire or flame
examples: The novel uses pyromachy as a metaphor for political conflict.
Legends describe ancient battles marked by pyromachy.

senses.definition: relating to both fire (pyro) and magnetism (magnetic); magnetic properties influenced by heat or fire
examples: The study explores pyromagnetic effects in certain materials.
Pyromagnetic sensors respond to heat-induced magnetic changes.

senses.definition: a person who practices or is skilled in fire magic; a fictional mage who controls fire
examples: The pyromancer conjured flames from thin air.
In the game, the pyromancer unleashes blazing spells.

senses.definition: plural of pyromancer; people who practice fire magic in fiction
examples: The order includes several renowned pyromancers.
Many stories feature rival pyromancers competing for power.

senses.definition: divination practices using fire; magical fire-based omens in fiction
examples: The oracle performed pyromancies to predict the future.
In the fantasy world, pyromancies reveal hidden threats.

senses.definition: the practice of predicting the future by interpreting flames or fire signals
examples: They studied ancient pyromancy scrolls.
Pyromancy was once considered a serious form of divination.
